title: Koenig Twisted Squares PASS: fundamental (d,e)=(2,1) — 3-4-5 triple, H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF=24
_fixture_purpose: Anchor fixture. The fundamental QA direction (d,e)=(2,1) gives triple (F,C,G)=(3,4,5). QA elements H=C+F=7 (outer square side), I=C-F=1 (inner square side, sign: I>0 → hyperbola). Twisted-squares identity: H²-G²=49-25=24=2CF=2·4·3; G²-I²=25-1=24=2CF. Divisibility: always 24 for primitive Pythagorean directions. The pair (I,H)=(1,7) is the Koenig I→H step: inner-square integer 1²=1 maps to outer-square integer 7²=49. L=CF/12=12/12=1 (minimal). 24L=24. (I²,2CF,G²,H²)=(1,24,25,49): these four values form a QA arithmetic sequence with constant step 2CF=24.

triple._note: F=d²-e²=3 (red quadrance=semi-latus), C=2de=4 (green quadrance=4×area), G=d²+e²=5 (blue quadrance=hypotenuse)

koenig_elements._note: I=C-F>0 → hyperbola; I=0 → parabola; I<0 → ellipse (QA conic discriminant)

quadruple_structure._note: (I²,2CF,G²,H²) form an arithmetic progression with step 2CF=24

koenig_chain_step._note: The Koenig series generates next direction from (d,e) → (H,I) = (C+F, C-F) = (d+2e+d²-e²/... algebraically: H=C+F, I=C-F as QA elements

next_triple._note: (7,1): F=49-1=48, C=14, G=50; note G=50=2×25 — composite, not primitive until reduced

algebraic_proof.div_24: For primitive Pythagorean direction (d,e) with gcd(d,e)=1 and d-e odd: C=2de (even), F=d²-e²=(d-e)(d+e). Either d or e is even (since d-e odd → exactly one even). If e even: 2|e → 8|2de=C; if d even: 2|d → 4|de and (d-e)(d+e) has d-e,d+e both odd with one divisible by 3 → 3|F. Either way, 24|2CF. ✓

validation_checks.description: F=d²-e², C=2de, G=d²+e² for declared triple
